description_long: This is a bulbous ceramic vase with a narrow, flared neck and two small loop handles on either side of its rounded body. The neck is decorated with alternating horizontal bands of dark brown and cream, while the body features a combination of geometric and linear motifs in earthy red, brown, and black hues. One side of the vase is more elaborately adorned with vertical stripes and chevron patterns, while the opposite side is mostly plain with subtle speckling. The surface appears matte with some visible texture, suggesting handcrafting. The handles are integrated into the body, providing both decorative and functional elements. The overall appearance suggests an artifact suitable for display in a public or semi-public urban setting, such as a plaza or park.
description: A rounded ceramic vase with a flared neck, striped and geometric patterns, and two small side handles. The surface is matte and decorated in earthy tones.
description_view_0: Front view shows the plainest side of the vase, with the striped neck and both handles visible.
description_view_1: This angle reveals one handle and the start of the geometric patterning on the body, with the striped neck prominent.
description_view_2: Back view displays the most intricate geometric and linear patterns, with both handles visible.
description_view_3: This view highlights the transition between the patterned and plain sides, showing one handle and the striped neck.
